deep_dive.title: The genetic signature of memory encoding along the human hippocampal axis
deep_dive.abstract: Episodic memory formation engages hippocampal oscillations that vary along the anterior-posterior axis, but the molecular programs supporting this physiological specialization remain unclear. Here, we leveraged a rare neurosurgical dataset in which patients performed verbal episodic memory tasks during intrahippocampal intracranial EEG recordings prior to en bloc hippocampal resection, enabling integration of encoding-related oscillatory signatures with matched cell-type-resolved transcriptomics from the same individuals. Subsequent memory effects (SMEs) spanned delta/theta, gamma, and hippocampal ripple activity across anterior and posterior hippocampus. Single-nucleus RNA sequencing from anatomically matched anterior and posterior tissue revealed longitudinal transcriptional gradients, most prominent in excitatory neurons. Spatial transcriptomic maps validated axis-enriched transcripts and their localization. Linking subject-specific SMEs to gene expression identified distinct molecular programs: anterior low frequency SMEs associated with synaptic and chromatin-regulatory pathways, and posterior high-frequency SMEs associated with metabolic and protein synthesis processes. Gene regulatory network inference further revealed axis-specific hub architectures. Together, these results define a cell-type-specific genetic architecture linking longitudinal molecular specialization to the human hippocampal encoding dynamics.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: intracranial EEG along hippocampal axis uses brain region priors
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 沿海马轴的颅内EEG使用脑区先验
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 沿海马轴的颅内EEG使用脑区先验
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 沿海马轴的颅内EEG使用脑区先验
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper integrates intracranial EEG recordings from anterior and posterior hippocampus during memory encoding with matched transcriptomics, revealing region-specific oscillatory signatures and their genetic correlates.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究利用罕见的神经外科数据集，在患者完成言语记忆任务时记录沿海马前后轴的颅内EEG，并结合匹配的细胞类型分辨转录组学。结果显示记忆编码相关的振荡特征（delta/theta、gamma和涟漪）在海马前部和后部存在差异，且与特定基因表达程序相关。工作揭示了脑区特异性生理活动的分子基础，为理解记忆的海马亚区功能分化提供了新见解。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该研究利用罕见的神经外科数据集，在患者完成言语记忆任务时记录沿海马前后轴的颅内EEG，并结合匹配的细胞类型分辨转录组学。结果显示记忆编码相关的振荡特征（delta/theta、gamma和涟漪）在海马前部和后部存在差异，且与特定基因表达程序相关。工作揭示了脑区特异性生理活动的分子基础，为理解记忆的海马亚区功能分化提供了新见解。
